cards.name: GOLDEN CHAIN deploy
cards.description: Rust + wasm GOLDEN CHAIN canvas builds in CI and ships to GitHub Pages. Software artefact only; carries no physics claim.

cards.name: 0 real Admitted (proofs/trinity)
cards.description: After stripping Coq comments and strings, the canonical proof tree proofs/trinity/ has zero real `Admitted.` This is a statement about proof obligations, not about physics.
cards.blocking_gap: Holds only for proofs/trinity/. Track B (proofs/clifford_cl8/) retains load-bearing Axioms with citations; the Lagrangian derivation is not closed.

cards.name: BT1-BT4 (Qed)
cards.description: Four Coq-formal boundary theorems: BT1 (no phi^a pi^b e^c fits Lambda + Omega_b), BT2 (no sigma-field from H4 alone), BT3 (600-cell D_F is vector-like), BT4 (2I-equivariant D_F cannot reproduce lepton mass hierarchy).
cards.evidence: proofs/trinity/BoundaryTheorems.v (BT1, BT2, BT3, BT4 all Qed)

cards.name: E8 eta non-convergence
cards.description: Wave 17 audit found that the E8-plumbing eta discrepancy does not converge to -2 and that the underlying B-matrix is heuristic, not a rigorous derivation. Listed as a boundary for this route.

cards.name: String/orbifold non-rescue
cards.description: No known string / heterotic / F-theory / orbifold compactification selects H4 or F4. A toy Z2 orbifold improves the Yukawa eigenvalue ratio about 2x against a required ~5000x.

cards.name: a4 reconciliation gap
cards.description: The `a4` conversion factor used in three different derivations does not reconcile end-to-end. Treated as an open gap until a single derivation is selected.
cards.blocking_gap: Produce a single derivation that all three (analytic, spectral, fit) paths agree on, or document why they cannot agree and downgrade the relevant fits.

cards.name: m_H = 4 phi^3 e^2
cards.description: Numerical coincidence m_H ~ 4 phi^3 e^2 lies inside the PDG 2024 window. The Coq interval bound is verified; the physical derivation is not. Tagged [phenomenological_fit].
cards.blocking_gap: Derive m_H from H4 / NCG structure rather than from a (phi, e) monomial. Any such derivation must pass the anti-numerology gate and avoid the BT2 sigma-field boundary.

cards.name: GOLDEN CHAIN framing
cards.description: Software artefact (verified): the GOLDEN CHAIN puzzle scores candidate boards against a fixed catalog and floors any board that contains a falsified tile. Physics meta-claim: a high in-game score is not evidence for a Theory of Everything.

cards.name: No ToE / no prize
cards.description: The project explicitly does not claim a Theory of Everything and does not claim prize-level recognition. Older documents that hint at such framings are retained for transparency and downgraded here.

cards.name: GF16 format spec
cards.description: 16-bit float with 1/6/9 layout and bias 31. phi-distance 0.049 vs IEEE fp16 0.118. GoldenFloat family (GF4–GF32) proven optimal via round((N-1)/phi^2). RTL multiplier and adder exist; 35/35 FPGA tests pass at 323 MHz on Artix-7. Layout identical to IBM DLFloat16 (ARITH 2019); semantic overlay (phi-distance, PHI_BIAS=60 rounding) is Trinity contribution.
cards.evidence: docs/hardware/gf16_spec.md §2–2.6; t27/conformance/FORMAT-SPEC-001.json; NeurIPS 2026 OPT paper (Prop 1–2, Thm 3); IBM DLFloat16 (ARITH 2019, layout identical, 6 differences documented)

cards.name: BPB phi-4Q advantage
cards.description: Synthetic phi-structured data shows 4-bit phi-ladder quantizer achieves raw BPB 0.125 (8x compression vs float32) and effective BPB competitive with 16-bit formats. [NUMERICAL_FIT] on synthetic data only.
cards.blocking_gap: Extend benchmark to real physics datasets (e.g. PDG parameter vectors, MNIST weights) and verify the advantage persists outside synthetic phi-monomial data.

cards.name: TTSKY26a tapeout
cards.description: Original single-chip GF16 submission (tt-trinity-gf16) on TTSKY26a. Merged in TinyTapeout sky-26a repo as PR #322. Silicon not yet returned.
cards.evidence: docs/hardware/silicon_anchor.md §3.1; PR #322 in TinyTapeout/tinytapeout-sky-26a for gHashTag/tt-trinity-gf16
cards.blocking_gap: Await silicon return from Sky130 fab (~Aug 2026). Post-silicon validation requires logic-analyzer reset witness, clock sweep, and conformance run.

cards.name: 0x47C0 anchor
cards.description: Claimed reset-time silicon witness of Lucas chain L2=3 via 0x47 on uo_out and 0xC0 on uio_out. Design is documented; no RTL evidence or post-silicon observation yet.
cards.evidence: docs/hardware/silicon_anchor.md §1–2; no RTL testbench found in t27 repo
cards.blocking_gap: Document the anchor in RTL (add reset witness to gf16_mul.v or wrapper), add testbench simulation, or downgrade claim to design-intent only.

cards.name: 0.694-bit phi step
cards.description: Mathematical identity: log2(phi) ≈ 0.694 bits. For a uniform phi-spaced quantizer, each additional level adds ~0.694 bits of information. Verified by pocket-calculator / Python derivation. Supported by three formal propositions from the NeurIPS 2026 OPT Workshop paper.
cards.evidence: docs/hardware/gf16_mathematics.md §3, §6.1–6.3; log2(phi) = 0.6942419136...; NeurIPS 2026 Prop 1 (Golden Self-Similarity), Prop 2 (Optimal Integer Rounding), Thm 3 (Universal Attractor)

cards.name: phi identity (binary64)
cards.description: Formal proof using Coq Flocq library that the IEEE-754 binary64 rounding of phi satisfies fl(phi*phi) = fl(phi+1) bit-identically. GF16 extension is open.
cards.evidence: proofs/trinity/PhiFloat.v (adapted from t27/coq/Kernel/PhiFloat.v); phi_sq_f64 = phi_plus_one_f64 by vm_compute; residual 0.0 < tolerance 1e-15
cards.blocking_gap: Extend to GF16: prove phi identity holds (or bound error) in the 1-6-9 format. Currently proved only for binary64, not for GF16.

cards.name: Three Crowns
cards.description: Three Crowns (Phi, Euler, Gamma) submitted together to TTSKY26b. 21 closed PRs in TinyTapeout sky-26b repo. Sequential validation planned: Phi → Euler → Gamma.
cards.evidence: docs/hardware/silicon_anchor.md §3.2; 21 closed PRs in TinyTapeout/tinytapeout-sky-26b (9 phi + 7 euler + 5 gamma)
cards.blocking_gap: Await silicon return from Sky130 fab (~Nov 2026). Post-silicon validation of all three crowns required before upgrade to verified.
